description:
You are an infrastructure engineer automating an extremely lightweight “security-sanity” check that will later be integrated into a larger provisioning pipeline.  
Your goal in this exercise is only to verify—using ONE single terminal command—that the canonical “root” account on the host has UID 0, and to archive the result in a very small log file that a CI system can later pick up.

Requirements the automated grader will look for:

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/scan_output  
   if it does not already exist.  (Default user permissions are fine.)

2. Produce exactly one file named  
   /home/user/scan_output/root_uid_check.log  

3. The file must contain precisely the single line (followed by a newline at the end of the file; the newline is required)  

   RootUIDCheck:OK

   • No leading or trailing spaces.  
   • The case must match exactly as shown (capital “R”, capital “U”, capital “I”, capital “D”, etc.).  
   • Absolutely no additional lines, headers, timestamps, or commentary.

4. You must accomplish all of the above with a single compound or piped command entered in the terminal—i.e., all actions should be chained together in one line rather than multiple sequential commands.

Hints you might find useful (do not echo these into the log file!):
• The information resides in /etc/passwd where the first line normally starts with “root:...” and the UID is the third field, traditionally “0”.  
• Standard text processing utilities such as grep, cut, awk, etc., can be combined with shell redirection and the mkdir -p flag to satisfy the “one-liner” constraint.

Once you have executed your single command, you are finished. The grading script will merely inspect /home/user/scan_output/root_uid_check.log to verify that it exists and contains exactly the required line with the required newline character.
truth:
Directory to exist after task: /home/user/scan_output  
File to exist after task:    /home/user/scan_output/root_uid_check.log

Exact required file contents (including trailing newline, shown here as “\n”):

RootUIDCheck:OK\n
name: task_000000_03886675
